steps.title: Track Oscar's reaction
steps.content: Oscar rises and asks which wonderful words the woman means, showing sudden interest. When he learns the words belong to Mr. Wills, he loses interest and returns to writing, so the underlined action matters because of that brief expectation.

steps.title: Connect the reversal
steps.content: Choice A invents an expected visit. Choice B overstates annoyance before Oscar knows why she is there. Choice C misreads his later loss of interest as professionalism. The underlined portion shows hope that she is praising his writing. The answer is D.
